GitClaw created or reused a durable GitHub channel room, then queued one provider-facing invitation per reviewed route. The room issue contains the human-readable topic and notes; this source receipt keeps route names, room IDs, topic text, notes, thread IDs, message IDs, and outbound bodies out of band.

### Destinations
- destination=`01` target_issue=`#565` outbound_comment_id=`4594452768` target_issue_created=`true` duplicate_suppressed=`false` route_sha256_12=`ff6bd0e4b42c` channel=`slack` thread_id_sha256_12=`5c959283d728` message_id_sha256_12=`fe48a05161fc` body_sha256_12=`d2451ccd2ce7`
- destination=`02` target_issue=`#566` outbound_comment_id=`4594453170` target_issue_created=`true` duplicate_suppressed=`false` route_sha256_12=`325d7a85c564` channel=`telegram` thread_id_sha256_12=`58f6c42669a7` message_id_sha256_12=`fe48a05161fc` body_sha256_12=`d2451ccd2ce7`

### Follow-Up Delivery
- provider gateways read pending room invites with `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>`
- provider gateways record sent room invites with `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>`
- duplicate room invites are suppressed independently for each route by `channel + message_id`
- normal GitClaw conversation continues on the room issue with the `gitclaw` label
